audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer parameters in delete.c are protected by API contract (assert-guarded in ldap_delete_ext at L101-104) and the sole server-side caller (back-asyncmeta/delete.c:75) explicitly null-checks msc->msc_ld before the call. The dn parameter flows to ber_put_string->strlen in liblber/encode.c, but no external LDAP code path can inject a NULL dn: LDAP protocol always includes a DN in delete requests with an allocated bv_val (empty string '' for zero-length, not NULL). No reachable attack path exists where a network attacker causes a null dereference within delete.c.
